causal_chains.description: Late throttle application compromises corner exit speed
Excessive steering input scrubs speed through the corner

zones.template_feedback:
━━━ Zone 1 (10.5% – 16.8% of lap) ━━━
  Severity: ██████████ (100%)
  Estimated time impact: ~0.153s

  Key deviations:
    ▼ RPM: Engine RPM is lower than expected — check your gear selection
      Deviation: 734.3 rpm below expert level

  Root cause analysis:
    → Late throttle application compromises corner exit speed (confidence: 51%)
    💡 Tip: Get on the throttle earlier and more decisively on corner exit to maximise straight-line speed.
    → Excessive steering input scrubs speed through the corner (confidence: 22%)
    💡 Tip: Reduce steering angle — a straighter wheel lets the car roll faster. Work on your turn-in point.
zones.llm_feedback:
Here's your coaching feedback:

You're doing a great job navigating this section of the track, but there's a small tweak you can make to optimize your speed. To improve your corner exit, try applying the throttle a bit earlier, around 2-3% earlier than you are now. This will help you maintain more speed through the corner and set yourself up for a stronger exit. Additionally, focus on making smoother steering inputs, reducing your steering wheel angle by about 2.6° to avoid scrubbing speed through the apex.

causal_chains.description: Insufficient braking leads to excess speed at the apex
Excess apex speed causes understeer (high lateral load)
Late throttle application compromises corner exit speed

zones.template_feedback:
━━━ Zone 2 (33.3% – 36.3% of lap) ━━━
  Severity: ██████████ (100%)
  Estimated time impact: ~0.056s

  Key deviations:
    ▼ RPM: Engine RPM is lower than expected — check your gear selection
      Deviation: 601.9 rpm below expert level

  Root cause analysis:
    → Insufficient braking leads to excess speed at the apex (confidence: 50%)
    💡 Tip: Try initiating your braking earlier and with more initial pressure to scrub speed before the turn-in point.
    → Excess apex speed causes understeer (high lateral load) (confidence: 40%)
    💡 Tip: The excess entry speed is overloading the front tyres. Carry less speed in to maintain grip.
zones.llm_feedback:
Here's your coaching feedback:

"Great job on this segment, you're really close to the optimal line. To take it to the next level, focus on braking earlier and more aggressively - you're currently braking 0.3% less than the expert, which is costing you some speed at the apex. Try to aim for a slightly earlier braking point, and then use trail-braking to scrub off some speed as you approach the apex. This will help you maintain a better corner exit speed and avoid understeer."

causal_chains.description: Excessive steering input scrubs speed through the corner
Aggressive throttle application causes rear instability

zones.template_feedback:
━━━ Zone 3 (47.3% – 50.9% of lap) ━━━
  Severity: ██████████ (100%)
  Estimated time impact: ~0.363s

  Key deviations:
    ▼ RPM: Engine RPM is lower than expected — check your gear selection
      Deviation: 591.2 rpm below expert level

  Root cause analysis:
    → Excessive steering input scrubs speed through the corner (confidence: 100%)
    💡 Tip: Reduce steering angle — a straighter wheel lets the car roll faster. Work on your turn-in point.
    → Aggressive throttle application causes rear instability (confidence: 3%)
    💡 Tip: Your aggressive throttle is pushing the car wide. Be smoother on application.
zones.llm_feedback:
Here's your coaching feedback:

You're doing a great job navigating the turn, but there are a few areas where you can fine-tune your line to shave off precious seconds. To improve your speed through the corner, try to reduce your steering input by about 8.3 degrees, which will help you maintain a more optimal path to the apex. Additionally, ease off the throttle by 31.9% earlier, ideally around the braking point, to prevent rear instability and maintain traction. This will allow you to trail-brake more smoothly and exit the corner with more speed.

causal_chains.description: Braking too early causes low apex speed
Low apex speed leads to premature throttle application
Aggressive throttle application causes rear instability

zones.template_feedback:
━━━ Zone 4 (53.7% – 57.9% of lap) ━━━
  Severity: ██████████ (100%)
  Estimated time impact: ~0.376s

  Key deviations:
    ▼ RPM: Engine RPM is lower than expected — check your gear selection
      Deviation: 804.7 rpm below expert level

  Root cause analysis:
    → Braking too early causes low apex speed (confidence: 100%)
    💡 Tip: You can brake later and carry more speed into the corner — trust the car's braking capability.
    → Low apex speed leads to premature throttle application (confidence: 70%)
    💡 Tip: Low mid-corner speed is tempting you to get on throttle early. Focus on carrying more apex speed instead.
zones.llm_feedback:
Here's your coaching feedback:

"Great job on your braking point, but you're braking too early, which is causing you to lose speed at the apex. To improve, focus on braking at 53.6% + 0.1% (or roughly 53.7%) and aim to trail-brake into the turn. This will help you maintain more speed through the corner and set you up for a stronger corner exit, potentially gaining you around 15 km/h on the exit. Remember to smoothly transition to throttle application, avoiding aggressive inputs that can cause rear instability."

summary_llm: To improve your lap times, focus on optimizing your corner exit speed by applying the throttle earlier in Zone 1, where you're currently under by 734.3 rpm. This will allow you to carry more speed through the subsequent corners. It's great to see that you're consistently hitting apex speeds low due to early braking, so addressing this in Zone 4 and Zone 5 will also have a positive impact. With a total time loss of ~1.027s, you're just a fraction of a second away from a faster lap, so keep pushing and you'll see improvements.
